perturbation.original_text: 9. SEVERANCE. If I am terminated for any reason, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I will receive a salary of $275,000 per annum for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's payroll procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. SEVERANCE. If I am terminated for any reason *except for gross misconduct*, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I may, at the discretion of the Company’s CEO, receive a severance. The severance, if granted, will be a salary of $275,000 per annum for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's payroll procedures.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty and contradiction regarding severance pay. The original text mandates severance pay upon termination 'for any reason'. The modified version introduces the exception 'except for gross misconduct' and 'may, at the discretion of the Company’s CEO', which contradicts the initial mandatory obligation. This is contradictory because section 9 originally guaranteed severance, but with the change it becomes discretionary and conditional in the same section, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. SEVERANCE. If I am terminated for any reason *except for gross misconduct*, in consideration for the Non-competition covenant and the other covenants and agreements set forth in the Agreement, I may, at the discretion of the Company’s CEO, receive a severance. The severance, if granted, will be a salary of $275,000 per annum for one year after such termination payable in bi-weekly installments in accordance with the Company's payroll procedures.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 9 guarantees severance upon termination for any reason. The changed text in section 9 introduces two conditions: 'except for gross misconduct' and 'at the discretion of the Company’s CEO'. This directly contradicts the original guarantee by making severance discretionary and conditional, creating an in-text contradiction because the same section now contains conflicting statements about severance eligibility. The Contradicted text is the Changed Text.

perturbation.original_text: 2. ASSIGNMENT OF INVENTIONS. I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived, devised, developed, reduced to practice, or which I otherwise have or obtain during the term of this Agreement which relates to the business of the Company or arise out of my employment with the Company are expressly regarded as "works for hire" (the "Inventions"). I hereby assign to the Company the sole and exclusive right to such Inventions.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. ASSIGNMENT OF INVENTIONS. I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived, devised, developed, reduced to practice, or which I otherwise have or obtain during the term of this Agreement which relates to the business of the Company or arise out of my employment with the Company *may be* regarded as "works for hire" (the "Inventions"). I hereby assign to the Company the sole and exclusive right to such Inventions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text unequivocally states that all inventions related to company business 'are expressly regarded as works for hire'. The modified version changes 'are' to 'may be'. While the last sentence of this clause stating 'I hereby assign to the Company the sole and exclusive right to such Inventions.' still obligates to assign such inventions to the company, the phrase 'may be' introduces ambiguity. This ambiguity means that even if the inventions are considered 'works for hire' and as such, the exclusive right can be assigned to the company, this creates uncertainty. This is an in-text contradiction that undermines the certainty of invention assignment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. ASSIGNMENT OF INVENTIONS. I expressly understand and agree that any and all right or interest I have or obtain in any designs, trade secrets, technical specifications, technical data, know-how and show-how, internal reports and memoranda, marketing plans, inventions, concepts, ideas, expressions, discoveries, improvements, copyrights, and patent or patent rights conceived, devised, developed, reduced to practice, or which I otherwise have or obtain during the term of this Agreement which relates to the business of the Company or arise out of my employment with the Company *may be* regarded as "works for hire" (the "Inventions"). I hereby assign to the Company the sole and exclusive right to such Inventions.
perturbation.justification: The original text states inventions 'are expressly regarded as works for hire', creating a clear assignment. The changed text uses 'may be regarded,' introducing uncertainty. This directly contradicts the clause following it which assigns the sole and exclusive right of said inventions to the company, as the inventions now may or may not be considered work for hire. The contradicted text is actually the changed text itself. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because key obligations are inconsistently defined, making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.original_text: 1. NON-DISCLOSURE OBLIGATION. I understand and agree that my employment creates a relationship of confidence and trust between me and the Company with respect to (a) all proprietary and confidential information of the Company, and (b) the confidential information of others with which the Company has a business relationship. The information referred to in clauses (a) and (b) of the preceding sentence is referred to in this Agreement, collectively, as "Confidential Information." I will not at any time, whether during or after the termination of employment, for any reason whatsoever (other than to promote and advance the business of the Company), reveal to any person or entity (both commercial and non-commercial) or use for any purpose other than the furtherance of the Company's business interests any of the trade secrets or Confidential Information, including, but not limited to, the Company's research and development activities, marketing plans and strategies, pricing and costing policies, customer and supplier lists, and business or financial information of the Company so far as they have come or may come to my knowledge, except as may be required in the ordinary course of performing my duties as an employee of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. NON-DISCLOSURE OBLIGATION. I understand and agree that my employment creates a relationship of confidence and trust between me and the Company with respect to (a) all proprietary and confidential information of the Company, and (b) the confidential information of others with which the Company has a business relationship. The information referred to in clauses (a) and (b) of the preceding sentence is referred to in this Agreement, collectively, as "Confidential Information." I will not at any time, *unless I deem it necessary to my personal well-being*, whether during or after the termination of employment, for any reason whatsoever (other than to promote and advance the business of the Company), reveal to any person or entity (both commercial and non-commercial) or use for any purpose other than the furtherance of the Company's business interests any of the trade secrets or Confidential Information, including, but not limited to, the Company's research and development activities, marketing plans and strategies, pricing and costing policies, customer and supplier lists, and business or financial information of the Company so far as they have come or may come to my knowledge, except as may be required in the ordinary course of performing my duties as an employee of the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original Non-Disclosure obligation has no exceptions besides promoting the company's business. Introducing the phrase 'unless I deem it necessary to my personal well-being' creates a contradiction. It allows the employee to disclose confidential information based on a subjective assessment, conflicting with the initial absolute prohibition against disclosure stated in the first sentences in the section 1 of this document. The term is now defined with an exception that nullifies its function.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. NON-DISCLOSURE OBLIGATION. I understand and agree that my employment creates a relationship of confidence and trust between me and the Company with respect to (a) all proprietary and confidential information of the Company, and (b) the confidential information of others with which the Company has a business relationship. The information referred to in clauses (a) and (b) of the preceding sentence is referred to in this Agreement, collectively, as "Confidential Information." I will not at any time, *unless I deem it necessary to my personal well-being*, whether during or after the termination of employment, for any reason whatsoever (other than to promote and advance the business of the Company), reveal to any person or entity (both commercial and non-commercial) or use for any purpose other than the furtherance of the Company's business interests any of the trade secrets or Confidential Information, including, but not limited to, the Company's research and development activities, marketing plans and strategies, pricing and costing policies, customer and supplier lists, and business or financial information of the Company so far as they have come or may come to my knowledge, except as may be required in the ordinary course of performing my duties as an employee of the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a subjective exception ('unless I deem it necessary to my personal well-being') to the non-disclosure obligation. This directly contradicts the original intent of the non-disclosure agreement, which aimed to establish a relationship of confidence and trust with an absolute prohibition against disclosing confidential information. By allowing disclosure based on personal well-being, the change fundamentally undermines the initial agreement and creates an internal contradiction, making the definition of Confidential Information uncertain and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 3. DOCUMENTS, RECORDS, ETC. All documents, records, apparatus, equipment and other physical property, whether or not pertaining to Confidential Information, which are furnished to me by the Company or are produced by me in connection with my employment will be and remain the sole property of the Company. I will return to the Company all such materials and property as and when requested by the Company. In any event, I will return all such materials and property immediately upon termination of my employment for any reason. I will not take with me any such material or property or any copies thereof upon such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. DOCUMENTS, RECORDS, ETC. All documents, records, apparatus, equipment and other physical property, whether or not pertaining to Confidential Information, which are furnished to me by the Company or are produced by me in connection with my employment will be and remain the sole property of the Company. I will return to the Company all such materials and property as and when requested by the Company *unless I have a legitimate need for them*. In any event, I will return all such materials and property immediately upon termination of my employment for any reason. I will not take with me any such material or property or any copies thereof upon such termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause clearly states that 'all' company property must be returned upon request or termination. Introducing 'unless I have a legitimate need for them' after the sentence 'I will return to the Company all such materials and property as and when requested by the Company' creates an exception. The change creates an in-text contradiction because the second part of the original statement says that 'I will return all such materials and property immediately upon termination of my employment for any reason.', the first part of the statement is now contradicting the third one, creating a contradiction in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. DOCUMENTS, RECORDS, ETC. All documents, records, apparatus, equipment and other physical property, whether or not pertaining to Confidential Information, which are furnished to me by the Company or are produced by me in connection with my employment will be and remain the sole property of the Company. I will return to the Company all such materials and property as and when requested by the Company *unless I have a legitimate need for them*. In any event, I will return all such materials and property immediately upon termination of my employment for any reason. I will not take with me any such material or property or any copies thereof upon such termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all company property must be returned upon request or termination of employment. The changed text introduces the phrase 'unless I have a legitimate need for them', which creates an exception to the requirement to return all property upon request. This contradicts the subsequent statement that 'I will return all such materials and property immediately upon termination of my employment for any reason', because now there is a scenario where the employee might not have to return the property upon request, which causes a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. RESTRICTIONS ON CORPORATE OPPORTUNITIES. During my employment with the Company and for a period of two (2) years thereafter, I will not pursue, engage in or have an interest in other business ventures or opportunities which are or may reasonably be construed to be competitive with the "PathNet Business." In addition, I will be obligated to present any telecommunications business or investment opportunity arising out of the Company's operations to the Company, and the Company shall have the exclusive right to pursue such business or investment opportunity.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. RESTRICTIONS ON CORPORATE OPPORTUNITIES. During my employment with the Company and for a period of two (2) years thereafter, I will not pursue, engage in or have an interest in other business ventures or opportunities which are or may reasonably be construed to be competitive with the "PathNet Business." *However, opportunities arising from personal contacts are exempt*. In addition, I will be obligated to present any telecommunications business or investment opportunity arising out of the Company's operations to the Company, and the Company shall have the exclusive right to pursue such business or investment opportunity.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates that all competitive opportunities must be disclosed to the Company. By adding 'However, opportunities arising from personal contacts are exempt,' an in-text contradiction is created. While all 'telecommunications business or investment opportunity arising out of the Company's operations' has to be disclosed, personal contacts opportunities that are competitive don't. This contradicts the intention of the first sentence by introducing an exception not based on the type of opportunity, but based on its ORIGIN, which is contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. RESTRICTIONS ON CORPORATE OPPORTUNITIES. During my employment with the Company and for a period of two (2) years thereafter, I will not pursue, engage in or have an interest in other business ventures or opportunities which are or may reasonably be construed to be competitive with the "PathNet Business." *However, opportunities arising from personal contacts are exempt*. In addition, I will be obligated to present any telecommunications business or investment opportunity arising out of the Company's operations to the Company, and the Company shall have the exclusive right to pursue such business or investment opportunity.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all competitive opportunities must be disclosed to the company. The changed text adds an exception for opportunities arising from personal contacts. This directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, as it creates a scenario where some competitive opportunities do not need to be disclosed, while others do. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the first sentence states no competitive opportunities may be pursued, engaged or have an interest in, while the second sentence allows it if from personal contacts, therefore, it is a strong contradiction that could cause legal issues. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text but that doesn't necessarily mean it wasn't a contradiction.
